questions.question: What is the difference between a virtual function and a pure virtual function?
expectedPoints: Virtual functions can have implementations in base class
Pure virtual functions have no implementation and use = 0 syntax
Classes with pure virtual functions become abstract
Derived classes must implement pure virtual functions

questions.question: Explain why abstract classes cannot be instantiated and how this property is useful in software design.
Describe the Template Method pattern and how abstract classes facilitate its implementation.
expectedPoints: Defines algorithm skeleton in base class
Uses pure virtual functions for steps that vary
Provides default implementations for common steps
Allows subclasses to redefine specific steps

questions.question: Create a complete example of an abstract class hierarchy for a banking system with at least two derived classes.
expectedPoints: Abstract Account class with pure virtual functions
Implementation of withdraw() and deposit() methods
